repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 is a distinct proxy-null bridge-fiber problem.

published_prior_art.cite: tian_pearl_2000_probabilities_causation gives generic sharp PN bounds; dawid_murtas_musio_2014_bounding_pc_mediation uses mediator information under no mediator-outcome confounding.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal gives sharp PN intervals by deleting mediator-defier response types under arbitrary mediator-outcome response dependence.

published_prior_art.cite: murtas_dawid_musio_2017_new_bounds_pc_mediation and dawid_humphreys_musio_2024_bound_causes_effects_mediators study mediator-informed PC bounds under stronger process information.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 concerns proxy-null fibers and pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 concerns ordinal principal strata; neither contains a PN mediator-defier-face strictness frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: xie_li_2026_partial_causal_diagrams supplies a close mediator-informed PN/PNS program with back-door and conditional-independence constraints, but not an observable defier-face strictness frontier under arbitrary M-Y response dependence.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal supplies tie-aware inference for the paired sign-free/mediator-monotone PN endpoint map.

published_prior_art.cite: fang_santos_2019_directionally_differentiable supplies generic directional-delta/bootstrap theory, while zhang_wang_miao_2025_causal_attribution_confidence supplies close PN-bound inference.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question oeq:canonical-defier-threshold
soundness_flags.one_line: For intervals, “strictly contained” ordinarily permits only one strict endpoint, whereas T(p)>0 requires both S^-(p)>0 and S^+(p)>0; state “strict at both endpoints” or separately characterize proper containment.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem thm:pn-endpoint-limit / def:tie-aware-bootstrap-handle
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated bootstrap applies the unknown population derivative Phi'_p but defines no computable estimated derivative, resampling statistic, or tuning rule, so the claimed procedure is not yet a well-defined bootstrap.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem thm:pn-endpoint-limit
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The T-coordinate limit and bootstrap claim is conditional on oeq:canonical-defier-threshold producing the finite directionally differentiable representation of T.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional on that derived representation, the endpoint-and-threshold plug-in map has the stated directional-delta and modified-bootstrap route.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the ambiguous interval phrase with an exact two-endpoint assertion, and add a separate proper-containment characterization if that weaker notion is also of interest.
Define a sample derivative map using estimated active faces, bootstrap weights, and a Fang-Santos-compatible near-tie tuning sequence; state the resulting resampling statistic explicitly.

Add a nondegenerate binary worked law with primal endpoint couplings, positive values of both slacks, and a directly calculated relatively open neighborhood; contrast its constraints explicitly with Xie and Li (2026, Theorem 3.3 and equations (14)–(15)).

recommended_next_step: Repair the strictness terminology and define the actual tie-aware resampling statistic, then return the field-grade defier-face frontier for re-review.
